Catholic Church")  [Immaculate Conception Church, Lihue](/wiki/Immaculate_Conception_Catholic_Church_(Lihue,_Hawaii) "Immaculate Conception Catholic Church (Lihue, Hawaii)")  [St. Joseph Church, Makawao](/wiki/Saint_Joseph_Catholic_Church_(Makawao,_Hawaii) "Saint Joseph Catholic Church (Makawao, Hawaii)")  [Our Lady of Go

(also called simply **The Painted Church**[[1]](#cite_note-nris-1)) is a [parish](/wiki/Parish "Parish") of the [Roman Catholic Church](/wiki/Roman_Catholic_Church "Roman Catholic Church") in [Hōnaunau, Hawaii](/wiki/H%C5%8Dnaunau,_Hawaii "Hōnaunau, Hawaii"). It was built between 1899 and 1902 under the direction of the Belgian Catholic missionary Father [John Velghe](/w/index.php?title=John_Velghe&action=edit&redlink=1 "John Velghe (page does not exist)"), who then painted [frescoes](/wiki/Fresco "Fresco") along the interior ceiling and walls. An untrained folk-artist, Fr. Velghe depicted various biblical scenes, such as [Cain's murder of Abel](/wiki/Cain_and_Abel "Cain and Abel"), [Jesus refusing the Devil](/wiki/Temptation_of_Christ "Temptation of Christ"), and [the writing on the wall](/wiki/Belshazzar%27s_Feast "Belshazzar's Feast"), as well as a vivid illustration of sinners in Hell.[[3]](#cite_note-Williams-3) While the building itself is small and rectangular, Fr. Velghe painted Gothic vaults above the altar, creating the illusion of a European Gothic cathedral, inspired particularly by [Burgos Cathedral](/wiki/Burgos_Cathedral "Burgos Cathedral").[[1]](#cite_note-nris-1) Several other priests learned from Velghe and went on to establish other 'painted churches', including [Star of the Sea Painted Church](/wiki/Star_of_the_Sea_Painted_Church "Star of the Sea Painted Church"), painted by Father [Evarist Gielen](/w/index.php?title=Evarist_Gielen&action=edit&redlink=1 "Evarist Gielen (page does not exist)").[[4]](#cite_note-Mulholland-4)

[National and State Register of Historic Places](http://files.hawaii.gov/dlnr/shpd/architecture/reghaw-1205.pdf). [Hawai'i Department of Land and Natural Resources](/wiki/Hawai%27i_Department_of_Land_and_Natural_Resources "Hawai'i Department of Land and Natural Resources"). 2014-12-05.

Williams, Peter (June 27, 2000). *Houses of God: Region, Religion, and Architecture in the United States*. University Of Illinois Press. p. 284. [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [9780252069178](/wiki/Special:BookSources/9780252069178 "Special:BookSources/9780252069178").

Mulholland, John (June 10, 1970). *Hawaii's Religions*. Tuttle Publishing. p. 313. [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [9781462912537](/wiki/Special:BookSources/9781462912537 "Special:BookSources/9781462912537").

[Saint Benedict Church Parish Details](http://www.catholichawaii.org/parish-listing/saint-benedict-church.aspx). [Roman Catholic Diocese of Honolulu](/wiki/Roman_Catholic_Diocese_of_Honolulu "Roman Catholic Diocese of Honolulu"). Accessed 2015-1-01.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [St. Benedict's Catholic Church (Honaunau, Hawaii)](/w/index.php?title=St._Benedict%27s_Catholic_Church_(Honaunau,_Hawaii)&redirect=no "St. Benedict's Catholic Church (Honaunau, Hawaii)"))

Historic Place in Hawaii County, Hawaii

United States historic place

| Coordinates | [19°26′6.252″N 155°53′17.952″W﻿ / ﻿19.43507000°N 155.88832000°W﻿ / 19.43507000; -155.88832000](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=St._Benedict%27s_Catholic_Church_(H%C5%8Dnaunau,_Hawaii)&params=19_26_6.252_N_155_53_17.952_W_type:landmark_region:US-HI) |

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/ab/Inside_view_of_the_church.jpg/250px-Inside_view_of_the_church.jpg)](/wiki/File:Inside_view_of_the_church.jpg)

Interior of the church, showing frescoes

**Saint Benedict's Catholic Church** (also called simply **The Painted Church**[[1]](#cite_note-nris-1)) is a [parish](/wiki/Parish "Parish") of the [Roman Catholic Church](/wiki/Roman_Catholic_Church "Roman Catholic Church") in [Hōnaunau, Hawaii](/wiki/H%C5%8Dnaunau,_Hawaii "Hōnaunau, Hawaii"). It was built between 1899 and 1902 under the direction of the Belgian Catholic missionary Father [John Velghe](/w/index.php?title=John_Velghe&action=edit&redlink=1 "John Velghe (page does not exist)"), who then painted [frescoes](/wiki/Fresco "Fresco") along the interior ceiling and walls. An untrained folk-artist, Fr. Velghe depicted various biblical scenes, such as [Cain's murder of Abel](/wiki/Cain_and_Abel "Cain and Abel"), [Jesus refusing the Devil](/wiki/Temptation_of_Christ "Temptation of Christ"), and [the writing on the wall](/wiki/Belshazzar%27s_Feast "Belshazzar's Feast"), as well as a vivid illustration of sinners in Hell.[[3]](#cite_note-Williams-3) While the building itself is small and rectangular, Fr. Velghe painted Gothic vaults above the altar, creating the illusion of a European Gothic cathedral, inspired particularly by [Burgos Cathedral](/wiki/Burgos_Cathedral "Burgos Cathedral").[[1]](#cite_note-nris-1) Several other priests learned from Velghe and went on to establish other 'painted churches', including [Star of the Sea Painted Church](/wiki/Star_of_the_Sea_Painted_Church "Star of the Sea Painted Church"), painted by Father [Evarist Gielen](/w/index.php?title=Evarist_Gielen&action=edit&redlink=1 "Evarist Gielen (page does not exist)").[[4]](#cite_note-Mulholland-4)

St. Benedict's continues to be an active Roman Catholic church in the [Diocese of Honolulu](/wiki/Roman_Catholic_Diocese_of_Honolulu "Roman Catholic Diocese of Honolulu"), [vicariate](/wiki/Vicariate "Vicariate") of West Hawaii, holding five masses per week.[[5]](#cite_note-5) It is listed on the [National Register of Historic Places](/wiki/National_Register_of_Historic_Places "National Register of Historic Places"), and is open to the public for viewing seven days a week.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/9a/Painted_Church_127.jpg/250px-Painted_Church_127.jpg)](/wiki/File:Painted_Church_127.jpg)

Monument at St. Benedict's Catholic Church in Honaunau (Hawaii)

A monument for [Father Damien](/wiki/Father_Damien "Father Damien") stands in front of the church and is often decorated with [Leis](/wiki/Lei_(garland) "Lei (garland)").
